responses: Deepfake detection analyzes facial boundary artifacts, temporal consistency, and physiological signals like eye blinking patterns.
Our video pipeline uses frame-level forensics combined with audio-visual synchronization checks.
Diffusion-generated faces often exhibit unnatural hair-facial boundaries and asymmetric ear details.
AI-generated text typically exhibits low perplexity and burstiness compared to human writing.
We analyze token probability distributions, semantic coherence patterns, and stylistic fingerprints.
Our text engine uses ensemble scoring across multiple linguistic feature planes.
Image forensics examines noise patterns, EXIF consistency, and chromatic aberration signatures.
Diffusion models leave detectable artifacts in high-frequency components and texture regularity.
We perform error level analysis (ELA) and PRNU fingerprint matching where available.
Voice clone detection analyzes micro-tremor patterns, breath consistency, and spectral phase coherence.
Neural TTS often lacks the natural jitter and shimmer of human vocal fold vibration.
Our audio pipeline checks for artifacts in the 4-8kHz range where many synthesizers struggle.
Trust scores are calibrated using ground-truth datasets and Bayesian confidence intervals.
Attestation confidence combines model uncertainty with input quality metrics.
All scans include adversarial robustness checks against common evasion techniques.
